blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I stopped at that wall this morning. When I was running.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I do it every time now. I stop at the wall and I look at the fountain for a minute. I didn't tell you that.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I keep thinking — if I'd been five minutes earlier or five minutes later, I'd have been past it before you sat down. And none of —

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Small physics. I think about it a lot.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Me too. I was tired of going past things — that's what I said at the table. But I almost went past that too. I'd been sitting there for a few minutes before I noticed you were drawing. I almost moved before I looked up.
She was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Something about the way you were paying attention. Like you were doing something careful. And I wanted to see what you were being careful about.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: That's probably the best reason anyone has ever had to stay somewhere.
She made a soft sound that was almost a laugh but quieter.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You're good with words when you're not trying.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm terrible with them when I am.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I know. That's what makes the other ones so good.
